prompts.text: {player1}, take 2 sips.
{player1}, give out 2 sips — to whoever you want.
{player1}, take 3 sips.
{player1}, give out 1 sip.
{player1}, your drink is looking too full. Fix that — take 3 sips.
{player1}, take a sip for every ex you've texted in the last 6 months.
{player1}, name a country that starts with B. Can't? Take 2 sips.
{player1}, the person to your left decides how many sips you take. Max 5.

{player1}, what is {player2}'s middle name? Wrong — take 3 sips. Right — {player2} takes 3 sips.
{player1} and {player2}, rock paper scissors. Best of 3. Loser takes 3 sips.
{player1}, how many siblings does {player2} have? Off by more than 1 — take 2 sips. Exact — {player2} takes 3 sips.
{player1} and {player2}, staring contest. First to blink takes 4 sips.
{player1}, what city did {player2} grow up in? Wrong — take 3 sips. Right — {player2} takes 2 sips.
{player3} names any actor. {player1} and {player2} race to name 3 of their movies. Loser takes 3 sips.
{player3} judges: {player1} and {player2} do their best impression. Worse one takes 4 sips.
{player1}, what is {player2}'s go-to drunk food? Wrong — take 2 sips. Right — {player2} takes 3 sips.
{player1} and {player2}, thumb war. Loser takes 3 sips.
{player1}, guess {player2}'s birth month. Off? Take 3 sips. Right? {player2} takes 3 sips.
{player1} and {player2}, name as many Taylor Swift songs as you can in 30 seconds. Fewer wins — take 3 sips.
{player1}, what is {player2}'s most used app? Wrong — take 2 sips. Right — {player2} takes 2 sips.
{player1} and {player2}, arm wrestle. Loser takes 4 sips.
{player1}, what was {player2}'s childhood nickname? Wrong — take 2 sips. Right — {player2} takes 3 sips.
{player1} and {player2}, most dramatic reading of a cereal box. {player3} picks the winner. Loser takes 3 sips.
{player1}, what is {player2}'s dream vacation destination? Wrong — take 2 sips. Right — {player2} takes 2 sips.
{player1} and {player2}, best robot dance. Everyone votes. Loser takes 3 sips.
{player1}, how many years has {player2} known their best friend? Off by more than 3 — take 2 sips. Exact — {player2} takes 2 sips.
{player1} and {player2}, say the alphabet at the same time. First to mess up takes 3 sips.
{player1}, what was the last show {player2} watched? Wrong — take 2 sips. Right — {player2} takes 3 sips.
{player1} and {player2}, keep a straight face while the other has 30 seconds to make them laugh using only facial expressions — no touching. First to crack takes 4 sips.
prompts.notes: Replaced breath-holding contest (documented safety risk with alcohol)

prompts.text: {player1}, guess {player2}'s coffee order. Wrong — take 2 sips. Right — {player2} takes 3 sips.
{player1} and {player2}, 20 seconds of the best freestyle rap. {player3} picks winner. Loser takes 4 sips.
{player1}, what's {player2}'s hidden talent? Wrong — take 2 sips. Right — {player2} takes 3 sips.
{player1} and {player2}, take turns naming animals — no hesitating, no repeats. First to stumble takes 3 sips.
prompts.notes: Replaced paper airplane contest (requires unavailable materials)

prompts.text: {player1}, what was {player2}'s first job? Wrong — take 3 sips. Right — {player2} takes 3 sips.
{player1} and {player2}, draw each other as fast as possible. {player3} judges. Worse artist takes 3 sips.
{player1}, how many countries has {player2} visited? Off by more than 2 — take 2 sips. Exact — {player2} takes 2 sips.
{player1} and {player2}, take turns counting backwards from 50 by 3s. First to make a mistake takes 4 sips.
prompts.notes: Replaced spin-and-walk challenge (fall/injury risk with intoxicated players)

prompts.text: {player1}, what is {player2}'s biggest pet peeve? Wrong — take 2 sips. Right — {player2} takes 2 sips.
{player1} and {player2}, both say a number 1–10 at the same time. If they match, everyone else takes 3 sips. If they don't, both take 2 sips.
{player1}, what is {player2}'s go-to karaoke song? Wrong — take 3 sips. Right — {player2} takes 3 sips.
{player1} and {player2}, patty cake as fast as you can. First to mess up takes 3 sips.
{player1}, what does {player2} do for work? Wrong — take 2 sips. Right — {player2} takes 3 sips.
{player1} and {player2}, impressions of each other. Everyone claps for the best one. Loser takes 3 sips.
{player1}, guess {player2}'s shoe size. Off by more than 1 — take 2 sips. Exact — {player2} takes 2 sips.
{player1} and {player2}, take turns naming capital cities — no repeats. First to hesitate or repeat takes 3 sips.
{player1}, what is {player2}'s most embarrassing memory they've told you? Wrong — take 2 sips. Right — {player2} takes 2 sips.
{player1} and {player2}, who has more unread notifications right now? Check your phones. Higher count takes 3 sips.
{player1}, what is {player2}'s star sign? Wrong — take 3 sips. Right — {player2} takes 2 sips.
{player1} and {player2}, who can type 'the quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog' faster? Loser takes 3 sips.
{player1}, describe {player2} in 3 words. {player2} decides if they're accurate. If not — take 2 sips.
{player1} and {player2}, tell a 30-second story. {player3} picks funniest. Loser takes 3 sips.
{player1}, guess {player2}'s height in inches. Off by more than 2 inches — take 2 sips. Exact — {player2} takes 2 sips.
{player1} and {player2}, who can name more US states in 30 seconds? Fewer takes 3 sips.
{player1}, what song is most on {player2}'s mind right now? They rate the accuracy. Below 7/10 — take 2 sips.
{player1} and {player2}, wink at each other simultaneously. First to laugh takes 3 sips.
{player1}, what's {player2}'s go-to food delivery order? Wrong — take 2 sips. Right — {player2} takes 2 sips.
{player1} and {player2}, first to say the other's full name backwards wins. Loser takes 3 sips.
{player1}, how old was {player2} when they had their first kiss? Off by more than 2 — take 3 sips. Exact — {player2} takes 3 sips.
{player1} and {player2}, stand back to back and estimate each other's height. Closest wins. Loser takes 2 sips.
{player1}, what was the last thing {player2} spent money on? Wrong — take 2 sips. Right — {player2} takes 2 sips.
{player1} and {player2}, mirror each other's movements perfectly for 20 seconds. {player3} watches and calls out who breaks first. Loser takes 4 sips.
prompts.notes: Replaced hand slap game (physical contact injury risk with alcohol)

prompts.text: {player1}, what is {player2}'s biggest fear? Wrong — take 2 sips. Right — {player2} takes 3 sips.
{player1} and {player2}, each pick a secret number 1–20. Reveal at the same time. Furthest from 10 takes 3 sips. Tie — both take 1 sip.
{player1}, what was {player2}'s worst subject in school? Wrong — take 2 sips. Right — {player2} takes 2 sips.
{player1} and {player2}, air guitar solos. {player3} judges. Loser takes 3 sips.
{player1}, what would {player2} do with $1 million? They rate your accuracy 1–10. Under 6 — take 3 sips.
{player1} and {player2} — on the count of 3, both say 'not it'. Last one to say it takes 3 sips.
{player1}, what is {player2}'s most-used phrase or saying? Accuracy judged by {player2}. Wrong — take 2 sips. Right — {player2} takes 2 sips.
{player3} thinks of a word and gives one clue. {player1} and {player2} race to guess it. First to get it right wins. Loser takes 3 sips.
{player1}, guess how many photos {player2} has on their camera roll. They check and reveal. Off by more than 1,000 — take 3 sips. Within 100 — {player2} takes 3 sips.
{player1} and {player2}, who has more steps on their phone today? Fewer takes 2 sips.
{player1}, what is {player2}'s most-used emoji? Wrong — take 2 sips. Right — {player2} takes 2 sips.
{player1}, what was {player2}'s childhood dream job? Wrong — take 2 sips. Right — {player2} takes 3 sips.
{player1} is now the Thumbmaster. Anytime they place their thumb on the table, last to notice takes 2 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: {player1} is no longer the Thumbmaster. The crown has been retired.

prompts.text: No first names for the next {rule_turns} rounds. Say one and take 2 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: First names are back. Use them wisely.

prompts.text: {player1} makes a rule right now. Everyone must follow it for {rule_turns} rounds. Break it — take 2 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: {player1}'s custom rule is officially over.

prompts.text: No pointing for the next {rule_turns} rounds. Point and take 2 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: Pointing is legal again. Try to control yourself.

prompts.text: Every time someone laughs for the next {rule_turns} rounds, they take 1 sip.
prompts.rule_end_text: The laugh tax has been lifted. Laugh freely.

prompts.text: {player1} is the drink dealer for the next {rule_turns} rounds. When someone takes a sip, {player1} decides if anyone joins.
prompts.rule_end_text: {player1}'s power has ended. Everyone drinks on their own again.

prompts.text: No swearing for the next {rule_turns} rounds. Drop one — take 2 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: Swear all you want. Language restrictions lifted.

prompts.text: Everyone must refer to {player1} as 'Your Majesty' for the next {rule_turns} rounds. Forget? Take 2 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: {player1} is no longer royalty. They are just {player1} again.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, everyone must speak in full sentences — no one-word answers. Fail? Take 2 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: One-word answers are back. Nice.

prompts.text: Drinks must only be picked up with your non-dominant hand for the next {rule_turns} rounds. Wrong hand — take 1 sip.
prompts.rule_end_text: You can use your dominant hand again. Your weak arm thanks you.

prompts.text: No one is allowed to say 'drink' for the next {rule_turns} rounds. Say it — take 2 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: The word is back. DRINK.

prompts.text: {player1} chooses a word. Say that word in the next {rule_turns} rounds — take 2 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: {player1}'s forbidden word rule is over.

prompts.text: Everyone must whisper for the next {rule_turns} rounds. Normal volume — take 2 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: Volume restored. Speak up.

prompts.text: No phones for the next {rule_turns} rounds. Touch yours — take 2 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: Phones are allowed again. Check your notifications.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, everyone must compliment the person next to them before taking a sip.
prompts.rule_end_text: The compliment rule is over. Be mean again if you want.

prompts.text: {player1} is the official question master for {rule_turns} rounds. Answer their question without asking one back? 2 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: {player1}'s question master reign is over.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, stand up every time you take a sip. Forget — take 1 more sip.
prompts.rule_end_text: You may sit when you drink. The standing rule is over.

prompts.text: Every time someone says 'like' for the next {rule_turns} rounds, they take 1 sip.
prompts.rule_end_text: The 'like' tax has been abolished. Speak freely.

prompts.text: No one may use the words 'yes' or 'no' for the next {rule_turns} rounds. Do it — take 2 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: 'Yes' and 'no' are back in play.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, {player1} must approve every sip taken at this table.
prompts.rule_end_text: {player1}'s sip approval authority has expired.

prompts.text: Everyone must speak with an accent of {player1}'s choosing for the next {rule_turns} rounds. Drop it — take 2 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: Accents optional again. The dialect experiment is over.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, before you drink you must say something you're grateful for.
prompts.rule_end_text: Gratitude no longer required before drinking.

prompts.text: {player1} is the vibe curator for {rule_turns} rounds. If they say 'vibe check,' everyone must respond immediately or take 1 sip.
prompts.rule_end_text: {player1}'s vibe curation is over. Vibes are self-managed now.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, high five the person to your left before taking a sip. Forget — take 1 extra sip.
prompts.rule_end_text: High-five rule is done. Drink without ceremony.

prompts.text: Whoever makes eye contact with {player1} in the next {rule_turns} rounds must take 1 sip.
prompts.rule_end_text: {player1}'s eye contact curse is lifted.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, every time you take a sip, you must wink first. Forget — take 1 extra.
prompts.rule_end_text: Wink rule is over. Blink however you want.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, drinks can only be finished if you finish someone else's joke. Half sentences? Take 1 sip.
prompts.rule_end_text: The joke rule has been retired.

prompts.text: Everyone must use a fake name of {player1}'s choosing for the next {rule_turns} rounds. Use your real name — take 2 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: Real names reinstated. You are yourselves again.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, every sentence must start with the letter S. Wrong letter — take 2 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: The S rule is over. Start sentences however you want.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, everyone must clap once before they speak. Forget — take 1 sip.
prompts.rule_end_text: The clap rule is abolished. Speak unclappped.

prompts.text: {player1} assigns everyone a new name right now. Use the wrong name for the next {rule_turns} rounds — take 2 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: New names are retired. Back to boring real names.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, no touching your hair. Do it — take 1 sip.
prompts.rule_end_text: Hair touching is allowed again. You survived.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, everyone must end their sentences with 'allegedly.' Forget — take 2 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: The 'allegedly' rule has ended. Allegedly.

prompts.text: Whoever talks the most in the next {rule_turns} rounds (decided by group vote) takes 3 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: The quiet game is over. Tally is done.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, every time a phone goes off — owner takes 1 sip.
prompts.rule_end_text: Phone penalty rule is over. Notifications are free again.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, drinks must be taken with your pinky extended. Forget — take 1 sip.
prompts.rule_end_text: Fancy drinking rule is over. Be uncouth again.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, {player1} narrates everything like a nature documentary. Break character — take 2 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: {player1}'s documentary has ended. Nature is at peace.

prompts.text: {player1} can point at anyone for the next {rule_turns} rounds and make them take 1 sip — once per round.
prompts.rule_end_text: {player1}'s finger of doom is retired.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, everyone must cross their fingers before taking a sip. Forget — take 1 extra sip.
prompts.rule_end_text: Crossed fingers no longer required. Drink straight-fingered.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, say 'cheers' before every sip. Forget — take 1 extra.
prompts.rule_end_text: Cheers rule ended. Sip in silence if you want.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, {player1} is the only one who can initiate a toast.
prompts.rule_end_text: {player1}'s toast monopoly is over.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, everyone speaks in the third person. Break it — take 2 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: Third person speaking is no longer required.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, no one can say the word 'game.' Say it — take 2 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: The word 'game' is allowed again.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, before anyone asks a question, they must state one fact first.
prompts.rule_end_text: Fact-before-question rule is over.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, {player1} decides who sits where. Move without permission — take 2 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: {player1}'s seating authority has ended. Sit wherever you want.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, the last person to put their drink down after a sip takes 1 extra sip.
prompts.rule_end_text: Speed drinking rule over. Take your time.

prompts.text: {player1} is the mood ring. For {rule_turns} rounds, if they smile, everyone takes 1 sip. If they frown, they take 1.
prompts.rule_end_text: {player1}'s mood ring power has expired.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, you can only drink if you can rhyme your reason for drinking.
prompts.rule_end_text: Rhyming rules are done. Drink without poetry.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, no one may say their own name. Do it — take 2 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: You can say your own name again. Try it.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, {player1} is the official timekeeper. They decide when rounds end.
prompts.rule_end_text: {player1}'s reign as timekeeper is over. Time is anarchic again.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, everyone must do a little dance before taking a sip. Skip it — take 1 extra.
prompts.rule_end_text: Dancing before drinking is no longer mandatory.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, only {player1} can refill drinks. Everyone else asks them.
prompts.rule_end_text: {player1}'s drinks server role has ended.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, no one can speak in questions. Ask one — take 2 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: Question ban is lifted. Ask away.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, whoever checks their phone must take 1 sip.
prompts.rule_end_text: Phone check penalty is over. Scroll freely.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, {player1} gets to veto one drink penalty per round for themselves.
prompts.rule_end_text: {player1}'s veto card has expired.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, everyone must use a Southern accent. Drop it — take 2 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: Y'all are free of the accent rule.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, the last person to say 'floor is lava' takes 3 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: Floor is lava rule is finally over.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, no one may sit. Stand or take 2 sips per round.
prompts.rule_end_text: You may sit again. The standing protest is over.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, everyone must address {player1} before speaking. Skip them — take 2 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: {player1} no longer needs to be acknowledged. They're just a person again.

prompts.text: {player1} picks a topic. For the next {rule_turns} rounds, every sentence must relate to it. Off topic — take 2 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: {player1}'s topic rule is over. Talk about whatever.

prompts.text: {player1}, start: name a brand of sneakers. Go around — first to fail takes 3 sips.
{player1}, name 5 US states in 10 seconds or take 3 sips.
{player1}, start a category: types of pasta. Go around — fail and drink 2 sips.
{player1}, recite the alphabet backwards. Every mistake costs 1 sip.
{player1}, do your best celebrity impression. Majority thumbs down — take 3 sips.
{player1}, name 3 things in this room starting with S. Can't? Take 2 sips.
{player1}, start: name a country. Go around. Repeat or fail — take 3 sips.
{player1}, recite the days of the week backwards in 5 seconds. Fail — take 3 sips.
{player1}, start: name a car brand. Go around — first to blank takes 3 sips.
{player1}, name 5 Disney movies in 8 seconds or take 3 sips.
{player1}, say a tongue twister 3 times fast. Stumble — take 2 sips.
{player1}, start a rhyming chain with any word. First to break the rhyme takes 3 sips.
{player1}, name 5 countries in Europe in 10 seconds or take 3 sips.
{player1}, draw something on your phone in 20 seconds. Group guesses — if no one gets it, take 3 sips.
{player1}, start: name an animal for each letter of the alphabet. Stuck? Take 1 sip per missed letter.
{player1}, name 4 songs by one artist in 10 seconds or take 3 sips.
{player1}, name something for every letter of your name. One stumble — take 2 sips.
{player1}, start: brand of cereal. Go around — first to blank takes 3 sips.
{player1}, name the last 5 songs played on your Spotify or take 3 sips.
{player1}, say a 2-minute standup comedy bit. Less than 2 laughs — take 3 sips.
{player1}, name 3 things in your fridge right now. Can't? Take 2 sips.
{player1}, start a sentence. Each person adds one word going around. Whoever breaks the sentence takes 3 sips.
{player1}, name 5 animals that start with the letter C in 10 seconds or take 3 sips.
{player1}, start: things you find in a kitchen. Go around — first to repeat or blank takes 3 sips.
{player1}, hum a song. First person to guess it right gives out 3 sips. No one guesses? You take 3.
{player1}, name 3 types of cheese without repeating. Can't? Take 2 sips.
{player1}, list 5 types of cocktails in 10 seconds or take 3 sips.
{player1}, do 10 jumping jacks or take 4 sips. No half measures.
{player1}, start: famous people named Michael. Go around — blank first takes 3 sips.
{player1}, name 4 words that rhyme with 'night' in 8 seconds or take 2 sips.
{player1}, mime an activity for 20 seconds. Group guesses. Wrong? You take 3 sips.
{player1}, start: pizza toppings. Go around — first to blank or repeat takes 3 sips.
{player1}, name a song for every year in the 2010s. Stump a year? Take 1 sip per miss.
{player1}, start: things you take on a camping trip. First to blank takes 3 sips.
{player1}, say the months of the year in alphabetical order in 10 seconds or take 3 sips.
{player1}, name 5 superheroes in 8 seconds or take 2 sips.
{player1}, start: things you find in a bathroom. Go around — blank or repeat takes 3 sips.
{player1}, name a movie that starts with every letter of your first name. Stuck? Take 1 sip per miss.
{player1}, improvise a 30-second advertisement for a random object. Group rates it. Under 5/10 — take 3 sips.
{player1}, start: music genres. Go around — first to fail takes 3 sips.
{player1}, name 5 cheeses in 8 seconds or take 2 sips.
{player1}, start: things that are blue. Go around — first to blank takes 3 sips.
{player1}, name 3 songs from the 80s without hesitation or take 3 sips.
{player1}, spell the word 'necessary' correctly or take 2 sips.
{player1}, name 5 sports in 8 seconds. Repeat or blank — take 2 sips.
{player1}, start: types of shoes. Go around — first to fail takes 2 sips.
{player1}, do your best catwalk across the room. Group votes: fierce or flop. Flop — take 3 sips.
{player1}, freestyle a 4-line poem about the person to your left. If they hate it — take 2 sips.
{player1}, name 3 capitals of South American countries in 10 seconds or take 3 sips.
{player1}, start a categories round: things that are cold. Go around — first to blank takes 3 sips.
{player1}, take {sip_count} sips — no explanation needed.
{player1}, take a shot. Can't stomach it? Take 5 sips instead.
{player1}, take 4 sips. Or challenge {player2} to rock paper scissors — winner is safe, loser takes 6.
{player1}, take 5 sips. Sorry. Not really.
{player1}, you've been chosen. Take {sip_count} sips.
{player1}, take a shot or take {sip_count} sips. Your call.
{player1}, take 4 sips. The drink gods have spoken.
{player1}, the group has spoken. Take {sip_count} sips.
{player1}, take 3 sips now and 2 more on your next turn. You're pre-penalized.
{player1}, take 5 sips or let the group add a rule for you.
{player1}, take a shot or down half your drink. No sip option this time.
{player1}, take 4 sips. If you finish your drink, everyone else takes 2.
{player1}, everyone else nominates how many sips you take. Average wins. Min 3, max 7.
{player1}, take {sip_count} sips. Make it dramatic.
{player1}, the penalty card has found you. Take 5 sips.
{player1}, take 4 sips. {player2} can take 2 of them for you if they choose to.
{player1}, take a shot or take 6 sips. The crowd decides which.
{player1}, take {sip_count} sips. Don't ask why. Just drink.
{player1}, this round you're the target. Take 4 sips and give {player2} 2.
{player1}, take 3 sips. Then {player2} decides if you take 2 more.
{player1}, everyone points. If more than half are pointing at you — take 5 sips.
{player1}, take {sip_count} sips immediately. No commentary.
{player1}, you drew the penalty card. 5 sips. No debate.
{player1}, take a shot or let {player2} decide your fate (max 7 sips).
{player1}, take 4 sips and name one person who should probably drink with you.
{player1}, take {sip_count} sips and give a dramatic speech about why you deserve it.
{player1}, take 4 sips. If you're wearing shoes right now, make it 5.
{player1}, the universe has decided: take {sip_count} sips.
{player1}, take 4 sips or complete a dare suggested by the group.
{player1}, take {sip_count} sips. The house always wins.
That rule from earlier? It's officially dead. Carry on.
The reign of that last rule is over. Moment of silence... okay, drink.
Remember that rule? Good, because it's gone now.
Rule expired. The people rejoice. Someone take a celebratory sip.
That rule has been revoked. We move on. Together.
The rule is over. You're free. Try not to immediately break it out of habit.
Rule clock hit zero. Whoever hated it most — give out 2 sips.
The experiment has ended. Science says you survived. Take 1 sip.
That rule? Retired. We don't talk about it anymore.
Rule cleared. Next round, fresh start. Take a sip to mark the occasion.
Heaven! Last person to point up takes 3 sips.
{player1}, Would You Rather: drink 5 sips right now, or take a guaranteed shot on your next turn. You choose.
{player1} picks the next song. Everyone who hates the choice takes 1 sip.
The last person who arrived tonight takes 3 sips. You know who you are.
{player1} is now the Rule Maker. Invent a rule everyone must follow for {rule_turns} rounds. It goes into effect immediately.
prompts.rule_end_text: {player1}'s rule has expired. The people are free.

prompts.text: Waterfall! {player1} starts drinking. Each person starts when the one before them starts and can only stop when the one before them stops.
